title: Floating shade projector
abstract: A device is disclosed; said device is capable of floating in the sky by means of the interaction of its surface with the wind, as in the case of a kite. The device features a plurality of cylinders linked together, with valves at their frontal section in order to control the airflow therein. It likewise features a configuration of covers which close holes in the lower surface of the plurality. The holes may be employed to control the quantity of air within the device. The device is actuated by electric motors connected to a controller integrated in the device, no crew therefore being necessary. The invention may be connected to other devices by means of wireless networks, and may receive commands therefrom.
claims: Claims C L A I M S 1. A floating shade projector, characterised in that it comprises: - a plurality of identical cylinders laterally linked together, wherein each of the cylinders in turn comprises: - an upper surface, - a lower surface, - a valve, and - a plurality of holes defined in its lower surface, - a valve motor, - a battery, - a valve motor shaft to operate the valves. - a control box which activates the valve motor, and - a cable linking the cylinders to the control box. 2. A floating shade projector, as claimed in claim 1 , characterised in that it further comprises a plurality of covers in correspondence with the plurality of holes in the lower surface of each of the cylinders. 3. A floating shade projector, as claimed in claim 2, characterised in that it further comprises a cover motor for the activation of the plurality of covers. 4. A floating shade projector, as claimed in claim 1 , characterised in that it further comprises a barometer and a gyroscope. 5. A floating shade projector, as claimed in claim 4, characterised in that it comprises a microcontroller which determines the position of the floating device in accordance with a number of positional commands determined by the barometer and the gyroscope. 6. A floating shade projector, as claimed in claim 1 , characterised in that the plurality of cylinders further comprises a band for advertisements.
